title: Changed Functionality in the Database Connector Interface
breadcrumbs: Changed Functionality in the Database Connector Interface
text: Changed Functionality in the Database Connector Interface The Uniface database connector interface is very stable and changes to it are infrequent. The following table lists the major changes that connector developers should be aware of when updating existing connectors. Table 1. Changed and Obsolete Functionality Feature Type Explanation Uniface Release All connector requests, except for Logon, Logoff, Info Mode 0, Message, and Name table Connector Requests New return status ( -9 UIOSERR_LOGON_ERROR ). This indicates that the database connection has been lost. User-defined database connectors should be updated to handle this predefined Uniface error class. For more information, see the "Error Handling" topic. Uniface 8.4.06 and Uniface 9.1.02 Update Connector Request New return status ( -2 UIOSERR_OCC_NOT_FOUND ). This indicates that the specified record does not exist. Uniface 8.2.02 uicc.h Header File Renamed to iccapi.h Uniface 8 uxstring.h Header File Renamed to unistr.h Uniface 8 uiodef.h Header File Renamed to unidbm.h Uniface 8 uiflst->type Data Structure Field Renamed and retyped to unsigned short) uctrl->pack . Its contents are compatible with Uniface V7.2. Uniface 7.2 uctrl->uctrli Data Structure Field Obsolete since Uniface 6. All references to this field can be replaced with the reference to uctrl itself. Uniface 7 uloadcb() Service Function Obsolete since Uniface 6. All calls (address translations) to uloadcb() can be removed. For example: next = uloadcb(uctrl->uchfw) is now next = uctrl->uchfw Uniface 7 Related Links Error Handling Uniface provides an error handling mechanism that allows you to trap errors defined by Uniface, the connector, and the DBMS. uctrl Exchanges general information between Uniface and the connector, and describes a table.
